common.signDataFrom: Sign data from <aslLexLink>ASL-LEX 2.0</aslLexLink> (Caselli et al., 2017). Built by <deafenedLink>Deafened.org</deafenedLink>.

home.heroSubtitle: Pick a sign, watch the reference video, try it on camera, and get instant feedback on your handshape, movement, and location — powered by ASL-LEX linguistic data.
home.watchDescription: Select a sign and watch the reference video.
home.practiceDescription: Turn on your camera and sign it.
home.improveDescription: Get AI coaching on your form.
home.howMediaPipe: MediaPipe detects 21 hand landmarks per hand in your browser — nothing is uploaded to a server.
home.howAslLex: ASL-LEX 2.0 provides the linguistic ground truth: handshape, movement, location, and more for each sign.
home.howAiCoaching: AI coaching compares your detected features against the reference data and gives specific, actionable tips.
home.faqWebcamA: Yes — a webcam or phone camera is required for the practice feature. You can still browse signs and watch reference videos without one.
home.faqPrivacyA: Absolutely. Hand tracking runs entirely in your browser using MediaPipe. No video or images are ever sent to a server.
home.faqAslLexA: ASL-LEX 2.0 is a publicly available database of linguistic properties for thousands of ASL signs, created by researchers at Boston University and Haskins Laboratories. We use it to ground coaching feedback in real phonological data.
home.faqFreeA: Yes, the practice tool is completely free to use.
home.dictionaryTitle: Looking for sign descriptions and videos?
home.dictionaryDescription: Our ASL Dictionary has detailed articles for every sign, with videos, usage examples, and linguistic data.

practice.watchVideoPrompt: Watch the reference video, then start your camera to practice.
practice.cameraReadyHint: Hit <bold>"Record Sign"</bold> below your camera, then sign "{signName}".

camera.unavailableHint: Could not load the hand tracking model. Try a different browser (Chrome or Edge work best).
camera.noCameraHint: You can still watch the reference video and learn the sign. To practice with AI coaching, connect a webcam or try on a device with a camera.
camera.noCameraTip: Tip: This works great on phones — just open this page on your mobile device.
camera.blockedHint: Please allow camera permissions in your browser settings and reload the page. You can still watch the reference video in the meantime.
camera.errorHint: Something went wrong starting the camera. Please reload and try again.
camera.privacyNote: Your camera stays private — hand tracking runs in your browser
camera.cameraPrompt: Turn on your camera so we can give feedback on your signing!

onboarding.welcomeDescription: Here's how it works: watch a reference video, practice the sign on camera, and get AI coaching to improve your form.
onboarding.privacyDescription: Hand tracking runs entirely in your browser using MediaPipe. No video or images are ever uploaded to a server — your practice is 100% private.
onboarding.getStartedDescription: Click 'Start Camera' to begin practicing. Record your sign, then hit 'Get Feedback' for personalized coaching grounded in ASL-LEX linguistic data.

pathsPage.title: Learning Paths
pathsPage.subtitle: Structured paths to guide your ASL learning. Pick a path and work through the signs in order.
pathsPage.notFoundDescription: The learning path "{slug}" was not found.

pathDescriptions.first-20-signs: The most essential signs for everyday conversation. Start here if you're brand new to ASL.
pathDescriptions.fingerspelling-a-z: Learn the manual alphabet. Essential for spelling names, places, and unfamiliar words.
pathDescriptions.numbers-1-20: Count from 1 to 20 in ASL. Useful for ages, prices, addresses, and more.
pathDescriptions.family-and-people: Signs for family members and the people in your life.
pathDescriptions.feelings-and-emotions: Express how you feel. These signs come up constantly in everyday conversation.
pathDescriptions.animals: Fun animal signs — great for kids and visual learners.
pathDescriptions.common-phrases: Frequently used multi-sign phrases and expressions for daily interaction.
pathDescriptions.food-and-drink: Signs for meals, snacks, and beverages — perfect for mealtimes.
pathDescriptions.question-words: Master the WH-questions and other interrogatives that form the backbone of ASL conversations.
pathDescriptions.colors: Describe the world around you with basic color signs.

metadata.siteDescription: Practice American Sign Language with real-time camera feedback and AI coaching powered by ASL-LEX data.
metadata.homeTitle: ASL Practice — Learn Sign Language with AI Coaching
metadata.homeDescription: Practice ASL signs with real-time camera feedback. Get AI coaching grounded in ASL-LEX linguistic data. 2,500+ signs available.
metadata.browseDescription: Search and filter 2,500+ ASL signs by topic, difficulty, and more. Practice with AI coaching.
metadata.practiceDescription: Practice the ASL sign for "{name}" with real-time camera feedback and AI coaching. {details}
metadata.pathsDescription: Guided learning paths for ASL practice. Start with beginner signs, fingerspelling, numbers, and themed bundles.
metadata.privacyDescription: Learn how ASL Practice by Deafened.org handles your data. We don't store your camera footage, personal information, or practice history on our servers.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.yourPrivacyMattersText: ASL Practice by Deafened.org is designed with privacy at its core. We believe you should be able to practice sign language freely without worrying about your data.
privacy.cameraVideoDataText: When you use the practice feature, your camera feed is processed entirely in your browser using MediaPipe hand tracking. <bold>No video or camera footage is ever sent to our servers or stored anywhere.</bold> All hand-tracking analysis happens locally on your device.
privacy.aiCoachingFeedbackText: When you request feedback on your signing, only the extracted hand landmark coordinates (numerical data points describing hand position and shape) are sent to our server for AI analysis. No images or video frames are transmitted. These coordinates are not stored after the feedback is generated.
privacy.practiceProgressText: Your practice history, streaks, and learning path progress are stored locally in your browser (localStorage). This data never leaves your device and is not accessible to us. Clearing your browser data will reset your progress.
privacy.noAccountsText: ASL Practice does not require account creation, login, or any personal information. We do not collect your name, email address, or any other identifying information.
privacy.cookiesAnalyticsText: We use cookieless, anonymous analytics (PostHog) to understand which pages are visited and how the site is used. No personal data is collected and no cookies are stored on your device. Your practice progress is stored via localStorage.
privacy.youtubeNote: YouTube — Reference sign videos are embedded from YouTube. YouTube's own privacy policy applies to video playback.
privacy.mediaPipeNote: MediaPipe — Google's hand tracking library runs entirely in your browser. No data is sent to Google.
privacy.postHogNote: PostHog — Anonymous, cookieless analytics to understand page visits and site usage. No personal data is collected or stored on your device.
privacy.contactText: If you have questions about this privacy policy, reach out via <link>Deafened.org</link>.
